title: castalia pulchella
intro: Castalia pulchella, also known as the wild jasmine or fragrant water lily, is a species of aquatic plant that belongs to the arum family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Castalia pulchella include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Castalia pulchella can be made by infusing the plant's roots and rhizomes in water, and it can be used as a topical application or an oral remedy.
